think:
Let me analyze this new message and create a compartment for it.
Message 376 is a single assistant message. The user hasn't said anything new. The assistant is saying they整理了 session notes and are about to check the git state/diff/log before selectively staging files.
This is a very small, transitional message - the assistant is about to do git staging work. Let me consider what compartment this belongs to.
Looking at the session references, the previous compartment (352-375) was about "Finalized production implementation with deployment workflows and security hardening" and it noted "The actual git stage/commit/push was noted as pending at the close of this session."
Now in message 376, the assistant is saying they整理了 session notes (the session note from the previous compartment was written) and is about to check git state before staging. This is a transitional moment - the pending git work from the previous session is now being acted upon.
So this marks the beginning of the git commit/push work that was deferred from the previous session. It's a short transitional moment where the assistant is preparing to execute the staged changes, so I should extend the previous compartment rather than create a new one. The compartment ends at 375, and 376 is just the assistant saying they're about to check git state before staging. This is a transitional moment — the previous compartment noted "git stage/commit/push was noted as pending" and now the assistant is about to act on it. This feels like it should be part of the previous compartment's narrative, not a new one. The transition from "pending" to "about to execute" is part of the same work arc. I should extend the previous compartment to include 376, which is just the assistant announcing their next action without any user input or new substantive work. This is a transitional moment at the boundary between sessions where the assistant is preparing to execute the pending git work from the previous compartment. Since there's no new user message or substantive change here, I'll extend the previous compartment to include this message rather than creating a new one.

compartment.title: Finalized production implementation with deployment workflows and security hardening
compartment.p1:
Completed the remaining production implementation work. Async conversion of call sites proceeded with type fixes — the status route's missing `async` keyword was caught and corrected, and Firestore job store first-pass build errors were resolved. Request-id middleware and structured error logging were added to the API app.
Cloud Run deployment workflow was updated to include Firestore environment variables (`JOB_STORE_BACKEND`, `FIRESTORE_DATABASE_ID`, `FIRESTORE_JOBS_COLLECTION`). A Vercel web deployment workflow was added at `.github/workflows/deploy-web-vercel.yml` — triggered on push to master for paths under `apps/web/` and `packages/shared/`, pulls Vercel environment, builds the Next.js app, and deploys prebuilt artifacts using `VERCEL_TOKEN`, `VERCEL_ORG_ID`, `VERCEL_PROJECT_ID` secrets and `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL` var. A GCS lifecycle apply script was also included alongside the workflow.
PowerShell literal backtick issue was found in `app.ts` (literal `` `n``) and the file was rewritten in clean form. Session note `20260613_190000_firestore-job-store-deploy.md` was written to capture the full scope of changes. Final verification passed: `pnpm -r build`, `pnpm --filter web lint`, `lsp_diagnostics apps/api/src`, `git diff --check` (CR/whitespace only), and live API surface QA (health → ok, invalid upload → 422, HWP upload → expected converter configuration failure). All deployment configuration files were confirmed clean of PowerShell substitution artifacts.
The actual git stage/commit/push was noted as pending at the close of this session. Now preparing to check git state/diff/log and selectively stage only the intended files before committing.
compartment.p2: Async conversion with type fixes, request-id middleware + structured error logging, and deployment workflow additions. Cloud Run workflow gained Firestore env vars. Vercel web deployment workflow added. PowerShell literal backtick fixed in app.ts. All builds, lint, LSP, and live API surface QA passed. Session note written. Git stage/commit/push pending — now preparing to check state and stage intentionally changed files only.
compartment.p3: Production implementation finalized. Request-id middleware and structured error logging added. Cloud Run and Vercel deployment workflows completed. GCS lifecycle config added. PowerShell literal bugs fixed. Full verification passed. Git stage/commit/push preparation underway.
compartment.p4: request-id middleware; structured error logging; deploy-web-vercel.yml; Cloud Run + Vercel deployment workflows; GCS lifecycle
